etymology.description: 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") + ⺼(肉) (ròu, "meat") -> dog eating meat = "satisfied" -> 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") corrupted into 甘(甘) (gān) and later 日(日) (rì)
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a dog consuming meat to convey the concept of being satisfied. Over time, the components evolved, with the mouth radical being replaced by 甘 and eventually 日, reflecting changes in depiction while retaining the original notion of satisfaction.
